label: Information theory

node.label: Information theory
node.definition: Entropy, mutual information, coding, and rate–distortion quantify information, compression, and uncertainty.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Information theory is modeled as depending on Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Labels, targets, rewards & preferences, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Embeddings & distributed representations.
examples: atlas downstream link to Labels, targets, rewards & preferences
atlas downstream link to Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units
atlas downstream link to Embeddings & distributed representations
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: Information theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
